title: Lokanarkavu Temple
tagline: Lokanarkavu Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Lokanarkavu Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Durga devi situated in Memunda, 4 km from Vatakara, in Kozhikode District, North Malabar region of Kerala state o
overview.description: Lokanarkavu Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Durga devi situated in Memunda, 4 km from Vatakara, in Kozhikode District, North Malabar region of Kerala state of south India. Lokanarkavu is a short form of Lokamalayarkavu which means lokam (world) made of mala (mountain), aaru (river) and kavu (grove). The closest railway station is at Vatakara, which is 5 km from temple. The nearest airport is Kannur airport which is 54 km away.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Memunda
topPlaces.description: Memunda is a village near Vatakara town in Kozhikode district of Kerala, south India. The Lokanarkavu Temple is located at Memunda.

topPlaces.name: Villiappally
topPlaces.description: Villiappally is a census town in Kozhikode district in the Indian state of Kerala. Villiappally is the headquarters of Villiappally Panchayath, which contains two villages, Memunda and Villiappally.

topPlaces.name: Thodannur
topPlaces.description: Thodannur is a village in thiruvallur grama panchayath, Vatakara taluk, Kozhikode district of Kerala in India. Thodannur block panchayath consists of Ayanchery, Maniyur, palayad, kottappalli, thiruvallur, and villiappally villages.

topPlaces.name: Vatakara
topPlaces.description: Vatakara, also known as Vadakara (Malayalam: [ʋɐɖɐgɐɾɐ]) (formerly Badagara, French: Bargaret), is a municipality, taluk and a major town in the Kozhikode district in Indian state of Kerala. The municipality of Vatakara covers an area of 24.33 km2 (9.39 sq mi) and is bordered by Mahé to the north and Payyoli to the south.

topPlaces.name: Iringal
topPlaces.description: Iringal is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Sargaalaya Crafts Village
topPlaces.description: Sargaalaya is a 20-acre (8.1 ha) arts and crafts village in Iringal, near Vatakara in the Kozhikode district of Kerala, India. It was established at a cost of around 15 crores on 19 February 2011 by The Kerala Tourism Department.

topPlaces.name: Chennamangalam Temple
topPlaces.description: Chennamangalam Temple is situated in Chorode Panchayat of Vatakara Taluka in Kozhikode District of Kerala, India.The main Pratishtha is Lord Ayyappa. However Lords Shiva, Ganesh, Vishnu and Annapurneshwari are also worshipped in this temple.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Kozhikode
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Kozhikode, locally known as JNV Calicut is a co-educational, boarding school in Kozhikode district of Kerala state in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti—an autonomous body under the ministry.

items.note: Memunda is a village near Vatakara town in Kozhikode district of Kerala, south India. The Lokanarkavu Temple is located at Memunda.
Villiappally is a census town in Kozhikode district in the Indian state of Kerala. Villiappally is the headquarters of Villiappally Panchayath, which contains two villages, Memunda and Villiappally.

Thodannur is a village in thiruvallur grama panchayath, Vatakara taluk, Kozhikode district of Kerala in India. Thodannur block panchayath consists of Ayanchery, Maniyur, palayad, kottappalli, thiruvallur, and villiappally villages.

Vatakara, also known as Vadakara (Malayalam: [ʋɐɖɐgɐɾɐ]) (formerly Badagara, French: Bargaret), is a municipality, taluk and a major town in the Kozhikode district in Indian state of Kerala. The municipality of Vatakara covers an area of 24.33 km2 (9.39 sq mi) and is bordered by Mahé to the north and Payyoli to the south.
Iringal is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

Sargaalaya is a 20-acre (8.1 ha) arts and crafts village in Iringal, near Vatakara in the Kozhikode district of Kerala, India. It was established at a cost of around 15 crores on 19 February 2011 by The Kerala Tourism Department.

Chennamangalam Temple is situated in Chorode Panchayat of Vatakara Taluka in Kozhikode District of Kerala, India.The main Pratishtha is Lord Ayyappa. However Lords Shiva, Ganesh, Vishnu and Annapurneshwari are also worshipped in this temple.

Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Kozhikode, locally known as JNV Calicut is a co-educational, boarding school in Kozhikode district of Kerala state in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti—an autonomous body under the ministry.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lokanarkavu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lokanarkavu Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lokanarkavu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (63 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (136 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lokanarkavu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lokanarkavu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lokanarkavu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Lokanarkavu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lokanarkavu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Memunda, Villiappally, Thodannur, Vatakara, Iringal.
